title: Xtra Crispy Middle Eastern Eggplant Baba Ganoush
description: A cultured and crispy Middle Eastern dish perfect for a sick day, featuring palm sugar and nuka pickle, and is dairy free friendly in the form of baba ganoush.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Poke some holes in the eggplants and bake them for 45 minutes, or until the skin is charred and the flesh is tender.
Let the eggplants cool down, then peel and chop them into small pieces.
In a blender or food processor, combine the eggplant, palm sugar, nuka pickle, lemon juice, tahini, garlic, salt, and black pepper.
Blend the mixture until it is smooth and creamy, stopping to scrape down the sides of the blender as needed.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the blended eggplant mixture to the skillet and stir to combine with the oil.
Cook the mixture for 10 minutes, stirring frequently, until it is heated through and slightly caramelized.
Transfer the baba ganoush to a serving bowl and garnish with additional nuka pickle and parsley if desired.
Serve the baba ganoush warm or at room temperature, with pita bread or vegetables for dipping.
